title: UnitManager Mass Placement
summary: Use UnitManager for large batches of scenario object placement such as blockers, flags, markers, revealers, relics, tents, and world objects.
steps: Load or receive unit_manager from the scenario.
Group placement logic by gameplay area or system.
Use loops for repeated tiles or player-specific copies.
Keep placement code in setup modules instead of scattered trigger logic.
pitfalls: Do not scatter mass placement across unrelated feature modules.
Do not use trigger runtime logic for simple one-time setup placement.

title: UnitManager Region Cleanup
summary: Use UnitManager area queries plus remove calls to clean source-scenario content before new systems are applied.
steps: Define cleanup regions through trigger data or explicit coordinates.
Query the units in each region.
Remove the matched units in one dedicated cleanup pass before later setup systems run.
pitfalls: Do not interleave cleanup and feature placement if you can perform cleanup first.
Do not bury cleanup regions inside unrelated class or quest code.

title: UnitManager Filter And Convert
summary: Filter placed units by type or ownership, then convert or mutate them in a separate pass.
steps: Filter the scenario units by unit type and optional player ownership.
Apply ownership or state changes in a clear conversion pass.
Keep conversion logic separate from mass placement or trigger-state setup.
pitfalls: Do not mix conversion logic into unrelated trigger initialization.
Do not rely on ad hoc coordinate matching when type/ownership filters are sufficient.

title: MapManager Terrain Scan
summary: Use MapManager terrain iteration to detect terrain-driven setup rules such as placing blockers on black tiles or region-specific world content.
steps: Match terrain ids to the setup rule.
Place corresponding setup objects through UnitManager.
pitfalls: Do not merge terrain scanning with unrelated runtime gameplay logic.
Do not duplicate the same terrain pass in many systems.

title: MapManager Region Scan
summary: Use MapManager square-region helpers when the scenario setup depends on tile rectangles rather than single placed objects.
steps: Define the rectangular bounds for the region.
Read the region through get_square_2d or equivalent map-manager helper.
Use the tile data to drive later placement or logic setup.
pitfalls: Do not scan the same region repeatedly if one cached setup pass can provide the data.
Do not mix tile-geometry concerns into combat or quest lifecycle code.

title: Pass Parser Managers Into The Systems That Need Them
summary: Treat parser managers as explicit dependencies of setup or world systems rather than global state hidden across the whole project.
steps: Acquire managers once in the main scenario entrypoint.
Pass unit_manager or map_manager into the systems that truly need them.
Keep systems that do not need parser-manager access free of those dependencies.
pitfalls: Do not make every system depend on every manager by default.
Do not hide manager access as ambient global state when explicit dependency passing is clearer.
